CaseName: Kesavananda Bharati vs State of Kerala (1973)
Description: Landmark case that established the 'Basic Structure Doctrine' of the Indian Constitution.
Ruling: The Supreme Court held that while Parliament has the power to amend any part of the Constitution, it cannot alter its basic structure or framework.
Significance: Protects the fundamental features of the Constitution from arbitrary amendments.

CaseName: Maneka Gandhi vs Union of India (1978)
Description: Case regarding the right to travel abroad and the interpretation of Article 21.
Ruling: The court ruled that 'procedure established by law' under Article 21 must be just, fair, and reasonable, not arbitrary.
Significance: Expanded the scope of personal liberty in India.

CaseName: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy vs Union of India (2017)
Ruling: The Supreme Court declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.
Significance: Unified the jurisprudence on privacy and individual autonomy.
